description:
OBSERVED: closed mirror ball gets re-surfaced (moved closed → deferred) when its GH issue is deleted from the repo.

CHAIN: (1) ball delivered and closed; (2) GH mirror issue closed; (3) GH issue deleted (cleanup); (4) plugin's on_external_delete=deferred handler moves the *already-closed* ball back into the `deferred` bucket, where it shows up in `bl list` and demands triage.

OBSERVED IN mudbungie/balls: bl-f083, bl-6027 (mirror of canonical bl-2fe1), bl-bfe7/054c/2ee7 (mirror of bl-650b), bl-47f8 (mirror of bl-c972). All six were closed mirrors; deletion of GH issues #16–21 caused all six to surface as deferred in the next sync. Closed manually with `-m` pointing at canonical.

WHY THIS MATTERS / SEMANTICS: an external *delete* is being mirrored into what is effectively a *create* on the balls side (the closed ball gets revived into a state that demands attention). Delete and create are not symmetric operations the way close/reopen or title-edit/title-edit are. Mirroring delete back as a state transition that re-injects work is a semantic mismatch — the local side already finished the work, the remote side's housekeeping shouldn't re-open the question.

ALSO: bl-2202 (shipped 9abeaa2) fixed the *ingest* path so orphan-marker GH issues no longer create new balls. But it doesn't help here — the close→deferred transition fires on balls that already exist (created pre-fix or otherwise), and the on_external_delete handler doesn't consult the orphan-marker heuristic.

SCOPE / PROPOSAL: when on_external_delete fires against a ball that is **already closed**, the correct action is to drop the external mapping (clear `external.github_issues.issue.*`) and leave the ball closed — not to defer. Defer should only apply when the local task is still open/in_progress/review and the user might legitimately want to abandon it because the external driver vanished. Optionally: a config knob `on_external_delete_when_closed` (default `drop_mapping`, alt `defer`, alt `archive`) for users who want a different policy. Lower priority: same review applies to the orphan-marker case the bl-2202 fix detects on ingest — if a mirror ball survives from the pre-fix era, deletion of its GH issue should silently drop the mapping rather than defer.

ACCEPTANCE: integration test where a closed ball + deleted GH issue produces zero state transitions on the ball (or at most a notes/log entry); `bl list` stays clean.
title: on_external_delete=defer wrongly resurrects already-closed mirror balls when GH issue is deleted
